info.description: HRM API for Exnodes — see /docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-go-migration-design.md for the migration plan.
info.title: Exnodes HRM API

get.description: Returns every live label sorted by name ASC (no pagination).

post.description:
Idempotent. Case-insensitive lookup: returns the existing
label with HTTP 200 if one matches, otherwise inserts and
returns the new row with HTTP 201.
post.summary: Get-or-create an announcement label by name

get.description: Non-admins see only rows that satisfy the visibility predicate (published + audience match OR author). Admins see all.

post.description:
Saved as draft by default. Pass `status: "published"` in the
body to publish immediately (broadcasts via SSE). The Python-
parity shortcut `send_now: true` also works — when set and
`status` is not explicitly provided, the row is created
already published. Explicit `status` always wins.

`target_audience` accepts `all`, `department`, or `custom`.
`department` requires at least one `department_ids` entry;
`custom` requires at least one `recipient_ids` entry
(employee_ids).
post.summary: Create an announcement

get.description: Returns 403 when the caller cannot see this row per the visibility predicate.

post.description: Stamps published_at + broadcasts via SSE. No-op if already published.
post.summary: Publish an announcement

post.description: Records a per-user read marker. Second call is a no-op (preserves the first view time).
post.summary: Mark an announcement as viewed (idempotent)

get.description: Managers see all employees; non-managers see only their own row.
get.summary: Monthly attendance matrix (managers: all employees; others: own row)

post.summary: Admin manual create of an attendance row

post.description: Idempotent. Defaults the cutoff to now (company TZ) when omitted.
post.summary: Admin: close all open sessions before a cutoff (auto check-out)
Record a check-in for the authenticated user
Record a check-out for the authenticated user

get.summary: Export the monthly attendance matrix to Excel (all visible employees)
Export a single employee's monthly attendance to Excel

get.description: Managers (with attendance:manage_data) see all rows; non-managers see only their own.
get.summary: Flat list of attendance rows (Go convenience; not the BA matrix)
Get today's attendance status for the authenticated user
Get an attendance row by ID (owner or admin)

delete.summary: Admin soft-delete of an attendance row

patch.summary: Admin update of an attendance row

post.description:
Exchanges an email + password for a token pair. Required permission: `auth:login`.

**Brute-force protection.** After `MAX_FAILED_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS` (default 5)
consecutive bad passwords the account is locked for `ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT_MINUTES`
(default 15). During the lockout window, every login attempt — including the
correct one — returns 401 with `"Account temporarily locked. Try again in N minutes."`.
A successful login resets the failed-attempt counter.

**`remember_me`.** When `true`, the refresh token is issued with the long-lived
TTL (`REMEMBER_ME_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS`, default 30 days) instead of the
default refresh-token TTL. The access token TTL is unaffected.

**`is_active`.** A deactivated account is rejected with 401 *after* password
verification so the response cannot be used to enumerate which accounts exist.
post.summary: Authenticate and receive access + refresh tokens

401.description: Invalid credentials, account deactivated, or temporarily locked

post.description: Stateless logout — the client must discard its tokens.
post.summary: Acknowledge logout
Exchange a refresh token for a new token pair

get.description:
Paginated list with optional name search and parent filter ("root" returns top-level only).
Each item carries `employee_count` — the number of non-deleted employees whose
`employees.department_id` matches that department.

post.summary: Create department

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a department. Rejected with 409 if it has child
departments or any assigned employee. Positions are a flat
global catalog (post-migration 000014) and no longer block
department deletion.

parameters.description: department uuid(s) — repeatable, OR within filter
parameters.name: department_id

post.summary: Create employee (admin) — creates user + employee in tx

get.description: Active employees eligible as a line manager. Excludes the target (for_employee_id) and its transitive subordinate chain (cycle prevention); keeps a currently-assigned but deactivated manager visible.

parameters.description: exclude this employee + its subordinate chain
parameters.name: for_employee_id

patch.summary: Update current employee HR profile (restricted whitelist)

post.summary: Create a dependent for an employee

get.description: All live employees whose line manager is {id} — includes both active and deactivated reports.

get.summary: List skills assigned to an employee

put.description:
The whole skill_ids set is replaced atomically — passing
an empty list unassigns everything.
put.summary: Replace an employee's skill set (PUT semantics)

post.description: Generates a token, attempts to send the invite email, and returns the invite. SMTP misconfiguration is non-fatal — check last_email_error on the response to see if the email was actually sent.
post.summary: Issue a new invite

post.description: Token comes from the email link. Public endpoint — no JWT required (the token is the credential).
post.summary: Accept an invite — creates the user (public)
Resend the invite email (same token)

post.description: Accepts either application/json (body is the request DTO) or multipart/form-data with a `data` JSON field plus an optional `attachment` file.
post.summary: Create a leave request (optionally with an attachment)

get.summary: Get leave balance for an employee

parameters.description: calendar year (defaults to current UTC year)
parameters.name: year

get.summary: Get the current user's leave dashboard (balance + upcoming + history)
List the current user's leave history (paginated)
Get a leave request by ID (owner or admin)

patch.description: Multipart or JSON. Pointer fields in the body distinguish "not provided" from explicit values. If currently approved, an admin patch reverts status to pending (Python contract).
patch.summary: Update a leave request (owner-of-pending or admin)

post.description: Requires approve_team (own subordinate chain only) or approve_all (any employee).
post.summary: Approve a pending leave request

post.description: Cancel transitions pending or approved -> cancelled. Owner can cancel their own; admin can cancel anyone's.
post.summary: Cancel a leave request (owner or admin)

post.description: POST /leave-requests/{id}/delete (not DELETE) — matches the Python source. Admin may delete any status; non-admin owner only `pending`.
post.summary: Soft-delete a leave request (owner-of-pending or admin)

post.description: Same permission semantics as Approve — requires approve_team or approve_all.
post.summary: Reject a pending leave request

get.description:
Returns the latest 5 published announcements visible to the
current user, as `MobileAnnouncementBrief` items (description
omitted; fetch full detail via MobileGet). Unpaginated.
Mirrors Python's `GET /mobile/announcements/` contract.

Always visibility-filtered to published + audience match.
Description field omitted from each item; fetch detail via MobileGet.

post.description: Admin debug endpoint. Looks up the caller's registered device tokens and dispatches the payload to each. When FCM is not configured, every token counts as 'skipped'.
post.summary: Send a test push notification to the caller's devices

get.description: Returns the four attendance threshold fields (late/checkout, hour/minute).
Open to any authenticated user — the FE renders the map preview on shared screens.

patch.description: Stamps company_address_updated_at + updated_by whenever any of the three address fields are supplied.

get.description:
Paginated list of the global position catalog. Each item
carries `employee_count` — the number of non-deleted
employees currently assigned to that position.

post.description:
Position names are unique across the whole catalog
(case-insensitive) among non-deleted rows. A duplicate
name returns 409.
post.summary: Create position

delete.description: Soft-deletes a position. Rejected with 409 if any employee is still assigned.

post.summary: Create role

get.description: Returns the structured permission catalog. Requires roles:read.
get.summary: List grouped permissions (for the role-creation picker)

delete.description:
Soft-deletes the role (name becomes reusable). Rejected with 400
for system roles and 409 if any user is still assigned.

get.description: Paginated, sorted by name ASC. Optional ILIKE search by name.

post.summary: Create skill (admin)

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a skill. Returns 409 with employee_count
in details if any live employee is still assigned.

patch.description:
Multipart PATCH. Omitted text fields are left alone; an
uploaded `icon` replaces the previous icon and the old
object is best-effort deleted from storage.

get.description: Long-lived SSE stream. Emits an `announcement_published` event whenever a new announcement is published. Clients should reconnect on disconnect. Auth token may be passed as `Authorization: Bearer ...` OR `?token=` query param (EventSource cannot set headers — REVISION NOTES #9). Sends a `: keepalive` comment every 30s to keep proxies from closing idle connections.
get.summary: Subscribe to announcement events via Server-Sent Events

parameters.description: JWT access token (alternative to Authorization header)
parameters.name: token

get.description: Paginated list with optional email search and is_active filter. Each item embeds roles and the employee summary.

get.summary: Get current user (auth profile + embedded employee summary)

post.summary: Change current user email
Change current user password
Register a device token for push notifications

get.description: Returns the user with roles and embedded employee summary.

patch.summary: Admin toggle is_active on a user

post.summary: Admin changes a user's email (employees parity #13)

get.description: Returns {success: true, data: {status: "ok"}} when the server is up.

emergency_contacts.description: Emergency contacts — full replacement list at create (employees parity #4).

experience_year.description: career-start year; range validated in the service (validateExperienceYear)

skill_ids.description: Skills assigned at creation (inline — Python parity). Empty/absent = none.

emergency_contacts.description:
Emergency contacts — self may manage their own list (pointer-to-slice:
nil = leave unchanged, [] = clear all, non-empty = replace the set).

Emergency contacts — pointer-to-slice partial-PATCH semantics:
nil/absent = leave unchanged, [] = clear all, non-empty = replace the set.

experience_year.description: career-start year; range validated in the service (validateExperienceYear)

skill_ids.description:
Skills replace-set: nil/absent = leave unchanged, [] = clear all,
non-empty = replace the whole set (inline — Python parity).

remember_me.description:
RememberMe, when true, requests a long-lived refresh token (see
REMEMBER_ME_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS, default 30 days) instead of
the default refresh-token TTL.

x-enum-comments.PermLeaveApprove: kept for backward compat; prefer ApproveTeam/ApproveAll

x-enum-descriptions: kept for backward compat; prefer ApproveTeam/ApproveAll
